name: Trend Discovery
summary: Collect live Reddit, FMKorea, and Pann candidates for shortform workflows.
description: Fetches and ranks candidate topics from live community sources so the workflow starts from real trend data instead of static prompts.

availability.note: This module is already active in the current workflow, but it has not been split into a standalone installable package yet.

name: Telegram Control
summary: Run shortlist, review, approval, and revision loops directly from Telegram.
description: Handles Telegram commands, inline callbacks, and operator review flow for candidate selection and script approval.

availability.note: This module is already active in the current workflow, but it has not been split into a standalone installable package yet.

name: Script Writer
summary: Generate shortlist summaries and review drafts with the selected LLM provider.
description: Turns selected candidates into shortform-ready review drafts, revise loops, and approval copies using OpenRouter, OpenAI, Claude CLI, or mock fallback.

availability.note: This module is already active in the current workflow, but it has not been split into a standalone installable package yet.

name: Asset Packager
summary: Turn approved drafts into reusable production packages and upload-ready metadata.
description: Builds narration, captions, prompts, upload metadata, and package folder outputs after a script is approved.

availability.note: This module is already active in the current workflow, but it has not been split into a standalone installable package yet.

name: YouTube Publisher
summary: Connect OAuth and upload approved production packages to YouTube.
description: Handles YouTube OAuth, upload request files, metadata, and final upload execution from the approved package path.

availability.note: This module is already active in the current workflow, but it has not been split into a standalone installable package yet.

name: Filesystem Tools
summary: Local file read and write helpers for Claude workflows.
description: Starter MCP package for local document and code operations.
